claims.claim: The Goose framework (arXiv:2604.02047) proposes that fixed-budget speculation trees should be anisotropic: reliable context-match tokens form a deep spine while lower-acceptance transition candidates are allocated as wide fallback branches concentrated near the root.

claims.claim: This paper reports a bounded mechanism study that reproduces and stress-tests the anisotropic-tree yield claim using a dependency-free Python simulator with analytic expected-value computation and Monte Carlo validation (200,000 trials per condition).

evidence_refs.quote: # Anisotropic Speculation Trees Under Heterogeneous Acceptance: A Bounded Mechanism Study via Monte Carlo Simulation

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: At the paper-reported median acceptance heterogeneity ratio of approximately 6×, the optimized anisotropic tree achieves a +12.08% expected-yield gain over a balanced mixed-probability baseline under a 63-node budget, with Monte Carlo error below 0.002 tokens.

evidence_refs.quote: # Anisotropic Speculation Trees Under Heterogeneous Acceptance: A Bounded Mechanism Study via Monte Carlo Simulation

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Gains are positive and substantial at ratios 8× (+21.78%) and 12× (+18.62%), but mixed or negative at lower ratios (−15.47% at 1×, −6.36% at 4×), confirming that anisotropy is not beneficial when source quality is homogeneous.

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The core mechanism claim is intuitive: when one candidate source is much more reliable, the speculation tree should invest depth along the reliable spine (since each additional accepted spine token compounds expected yield) while allocating breadth to unreliable fallback branches near the root (where the probability of reaching them is highest).
